steps.title: What is Reaction-Diffusion?
steps.context: Introduces the fundamental concept of reaction-diffusion systems where two processes compete to create Turing patterns.
key_equations: \frac{\partial c}{\partial t} = D \nabla^2 c
D_v > D_u \implies \text{diffusion-driven instability}
\frac{\partial u}{\partial t} = Du·∇²u - u·v² + f·(1-u)
\frac{\partial v}{\partial t} = Dv·∇²v + u·v² - (f+k)·v

code_functions.name: step(u, v, params)
code_functions.purpose: Conceptual pseudocode showing diffusion via Laplacian and Gray-Scott reaction

code_functions.name: PARAMS structure
code_functions.purpose: Parameter definitions with f, k, Du, Dv, dt values

viz.type: ASCII art diagram
viz.stack: Plain HTML/CSS, SF Mono font
viz.shows: System overview with species, diffusion coefficients, reaction network, and parameter ranges

test.assertion: Checks for presence of reaction-diffusion system diagram

steps.title: The Gray-Scott Equations
steps.context: Presents the complete Gray-Scott PDE system with detailed term-by-term explanation.
key_equations: \frac{\partial U}{\partial t} = D_U \nabla^2 U - UV^2 + f(1 - U)
\frac{\partial V}{\partial t} = D_V \nabla^2 V + UV^2 - (f+k)V

code_functions.name: grayScottDerivatives(u, v, lapU, lapV, p)
code_functions.purpose: Direct JavaScript translation of Gray-Scott PDEs

viz.type: ASCII art equation display
viz.stack: Plain HTML/CSS, SF Mono font
viz.shows: Formatted PDE equations with sign conventions and steady state analysis

test.assertion: Checks for Gray-Scott equation display with proper term labeling

steps.title: Diffusion: Fick's Law
steps.context: Explains pure diffusion through Fick's second law, demonstrating concentration spreading via the Laplacian operator.
key_equations: \frac{\partial c}{\partial t} = D \frac{\partial^2 c}{\partial x^2}
\frac{\partial c}{\partial t} = D \nabla^2 c
\nabla^2 c \approx \frac{\bar{c}_{\text{neighbours}} - c_{\text{local}}}{h^2/4}
c(x,t) = \frac{1}{\sqrt{4\pi D t}} \exp\left(-\frac{x^2}{4Dt}\right)
\Delta t \leq \frac{h^2}{2D}

code_functions.name: diffuseStep(c)
code_functions.purpose: 1D diffusion using finite difference Laplacian with periodic BC

code_functions.name: animate()
code_functions.purpose: Animation loop with 3 sub-steps per frame

viz.type: D3 animated line chart
viz.stack: D3 SVG, requestAnimationFrame, simControls
viz.shows: Real-time 1D diffusion of spike spreading as Gaussian

test.assertion: Checks for D3 visualization and animated diffusion display

steps.title: The Laplacian Operator
steps.context: Detailed examination of the 2D Laplacian using 5-point finite difference stencil with interactive exploration.
key_equations: \nabla^2 u = \frac{\partial^2 u}{\partial x^2} + \frac{\partial^2 u}{\partial y^2}
\nabla^2 u_{i,j} \approx u_{i-1,j} + u_{i+1,j} + u_{i,j-1} + u_{i,j+1} - 4u_{i,j}
= 4\left(\bar{u}_{\text{cardinal}} - u_{i,j}\right)

code_functions.name: idx(r, c)
code_functions.purpose: Periodic index wrapping for edge handling

steps.title: Reaction: Autocatalysis
steps.context: Focuses on chemical reaction component showing autocatalysis where V catalyzes its own production from U.
key_equations: U + 2V \xrightarrow{k_1} 3V
V \xrightarrow{k_2} P \text{ (inert)}
r = k_1 \cdot [U][V]^2 = UV^2
\frac{dU}{dt}\Big|_{\text{rxn}} = -UV^2
\frac{dV}{dt}\Big|_{\text{rxn}} = +UV^2

steps.title: Reaction + Diffusion Combined
steps.context: First complete Gray-Scott simulation explaining Turing instability mechanism from differential diffusion rates.
key_equations: \frac{\partial U}{\partial t} = D_U \nabla^2 U - UV^2 + f(1-U)
\frac{\partial V}{\partial t} = D_V \nabla^2 V + UV^2 - (f+k)V

steps.title: 1D Finite-Difference Laplacian
steps.context: Introduces discrete finite-difference approximation of the Laplacian operator in 1D using the standard second-order central difference formula.
key_equations: ∇²u_i = u_{i-1} - 2u_i + u_{i+1} (second derivative, O(h²) error)
periodic BCs: u_{-1} ≡ u_{N-1}

key_equations: Forward Euler u(t+Δt) = u(t) + Δt·f(u,v)
CFL stability r = D·Δt/h² ≤ 0.5
full GS reaction term

steps.title: WebGL Context Setup
steps.context: Establishes WebGL context initialization with proper extension detection, handling the differences between WebGL1 and WebGL2 float texture support requirements.
key_equations: WebGL2: gl.RGBA32F format available natively
WebGL1: requires OES_texture_float extension for float textures
Extension detection: gl.getExtension('OES_texture_float') returns null if unsupported

steps.title: Framebuffer Objects (FBOs)
steps.context: Introduces Framebuffer Objects as the foundation for render-to-texture operations, enabling GPU computation through off-screen rendering.
key_equations: FBO attachment: gl.framebufferTexture2D(gl.FRAMEBUFFER, gl.COLOR_ATTACHMENT0, gl.TEXTURE_2D, texture, 0)
Bind sequence: gl.bindFramebuffer(gl.FRAMEBUFFER, fbo) → render → output goes to attached texture

steps.title: Float Texture Formats
steps.context: Explains floating-point texture formats across WebGL versions, addressing precision requirements for accurate PDE simulation.
key_equations: WebGL2: gl.texImage2D(gl.TEXTURE_2D, 0, gl.RGBA32F, W, H, 0, gl.RGBA, gl.FLOAT, null)
WebGL1: gl.texImage2D(gl.TEXTURE_2D, 0, gl.RGBA, W, H, 0, gl.RGBA, gl.FLOAT, null) (requires extension)
Data packing: u → R channel, v → G channel, B and A unused

code_functions.name: selectTextureFormat(gl, isGL2)

steps.title: Ping-Pong Double Buffering
steps.context: Introduces the ping-pong double buffering pattern essential for iterative GPU computation, preventing simultaneous read-write conflicts.
key_equations: Frame N: read texture A → write texture B → swap
Frame N+1: read texture B → write texture A → swap
Buffer swap: [read, write] = [write, read]

viz.type: Pre-formatted ASCII timeline diagram
viz.stack: Plain HTML/CSS, <pre> element
viz.shows: Frame-by-frame visualization of ping-pong buffer swapping with read/write roles clearly marked

test.assertion: pre element present with ping-pong explanation

steps.title: Full-Screen Quad Geometry
steps.context: Explains how full-screen quad geometry enables GPU compute by ensuring fragment shader execution across the entire output texture.
key_equations: NDC space coverage: vertices at (-1,-1), (+1,-1), (-1,+1), (+1,+1)
UV mapping: NDC [-1,1] → texture coordinates [0,1]
Triangle strip: 4 vertices → 2 triangles → complete screen coverage

code_functions.name: createQuadGeometry(gl)
code_functions.purpose: Creates vertex buffer for full-screen quad

steps.title: Pass-Through Vertex Shader
steps.context: Demonstrates the minimal vertex shader required for GPU compute, emphasizing the role separation between vertex and fragment shader stages.
key_equations: gl_Position = vec4(a_position, 0.0, 1.0) — pass NDC coordinates through unchanged
v_uv = a_uv — pass UV coordinates to fragment shader as varying
Vertex stage: 4 invocations per frame vs Fragment stage: width × height invocations

viz.type: GLSL code with syntax highlighting and pipeline diagram
viz.stack: Plain HTML/CSS, <pre> element with color coding
viz.shows: Complete vertex shader GLSL source, attribute setup code, and graphics pipeline flow diagram

test.assertion: pre element present with vertex shader information

steps.title: Neighbour Sampling in GLSL
steps.context: Demonstrates hardware-accelerated neighbour sampling in GLSL fragment shaders using texture offset calculations and automatic boundary wrapping.
key_equations: texelSize = vec2(1.0/width, 1.0/height) — offset between adjacent texels
North: texture2D(uState, v_uv + vec2(0, +texelSize.y))
South: texture2D(uState, v_uv + vec2(0, -texelSize.y))
West: texture2D(uState, v_uv + vec2(-texelSize.x, 0))
East: texture2D(uState, v_uv + vec2(+texelSize.x, 0))

viz.type: GLSL code with 5-point stencil diagram
viz.stack: Plain HTML/CSS, <pre> element with ASCII stencil pattern
viz.shows: GLSL texture sampling code and visual representation of 5-point stencil pattern showing center and cardinal neighbours

test.assertion: pre element present with neighbour sampling explanation

steps.title: 5-Point Laplacian in GLSL
steps.context: Demonstrates the complete 5-point Laplacian computation in GLSL, highlighting the transition from sequential CPU loops to parallel GPU execution.
key_equations: ∇²u = u[N] + u[S] + u[W] + u[E] - 4×u[C] — discrete Laplacian formula
vec2 lap = N.rg + S.rg + W.rg + E.rg - 4.0*C.rg — vectorized computation for both u and v
lap.r = ∇²u and lap.g = ∇²v — dual-species computation

code_functions.name: 5-point stencil sampling
code_functions.purpose: Texture reads for center and cardinal neighbors

viz.type: GLSL code with vector operation breakdown

viz.shows: Complete GLSL Laplacian implementation showing vectorized computation and comparison with CPU version

steps.title: Full Gray-Scott Fragment Shader
steps.context: Presents the complete Gray-Scott fragment shader combining Laplacian computation, reaction terms, and Euler integration in a single GLSL program.
key_equations: du = Du × ∇²u - u×v² + f×(1-u) — complete u equation
dv = Dv × ∇²v + u×v² - (f+k)×v — complete v equation
uNew = clamp(u + dt×du, 0.0, 1.0) — Euler integration with stability clamping
gl_FragColor = vec4(uNew, vNew, 0.0, 1.0) — output to RGBA texture

viz.type: Complete GLSL fragment shader with annotation
viz.stack: Plain HTML/CSS, <pre> element with syntax highlighting
viz.shows: Full shader source code with commented sections showing pipeline stages and parallel execution scale

test.assertion: pre element present with complete fragment shader

steps.title: Float Precision and RGBA Encoding
steps.context: Addresses floating-point precision requirements and encoding strategies across different WebGL versions and hardware capabilities.
key_equations: RGBA32F memory: 512×512×4×4 bytes = 4.2 MB per texture
RGBA8 memory: 512×512×4×1 bytes = 1.0 MB per texture
Float32 precision: 23-bit mantissa ≈ 7 decimal digits
Encoding scheme: u → R channel, v → G channel, B and A unused

viz.type: Format comparison table with memory analysis
viz.stack: Plain HTML/CSS, <pre> element
viz.shows: Side-by-side comparison of RGBA32F vs RGBA8 approaches, precision analysis, and memory usage calculations

test.assertion: pre element present with float precision explanation

steps.title: Visualization Pass: Screen Output
steps.context: Establishes the two-pass GPU architecture fundamental to real-time scientific visualization, separating computation from display rendering.
key_equations: SimShader: Gray-Scott PDE → new (u,v) state
VizShader: (u,v) texture → RGB display
gl_FragColor = vec4(visualize(u,v), 1.0) — visualization transformation

steps.title: First GPU Simulation: Correctness Check
steps.context: Establishes validation methodology for GPU implementations by comparing against trusted CPU reference calculations.
key_equations: Error metric: max(|GPU[i,j] - CPU[i,j]|) for all cells
Acceptable threshold: max_error < 1e-4
Validation: identical initial conditions, same parameters, same timesteps

steps.title: The Simulation Loop: rAF and Sub-Stepping
steps.context: Introduces the requestAnimationFrame loop architecture with sub-stepping to balance visual responsiveness with simulation speed.
key_equations: Pattern formation time: ~5000 Gray-Scott time units
Wall-clock time at 1 step/frame: 5000/60 = 83 seconds
Wall-clock time at 8 steps/frame: 5000/(60×8) = 10.4 seconds
Frame budget: 16.67ms at 60fps

steps.context: Introduces temporal blending to visualize pattern motion through frame-to-frame accumulation.
key_equations: Temporal blend: newFrame = α × current + (1-α) × previous
Trail length: longer trails with smaller α (more history retention)
Static patterns: unaffected by temporal blending

Sobel Y: [-1,-2,-1; 0,0,0; 1,2,1] × U — vertical edge detection
Edge magnitude: sqrt(Gx² + Gy²) — combined edge strength

Contour spacing: lines at U = 0.1, 0.2, 0.3, ... 0.9
GLSL implementation: fract(U × contourLevels) creates repeated boundaries

Zoom factor: scale > 1.0 → zoom in, scale < 1.0 → zoom out
Pan offset: uv_offset shifts visible region

Export process: WebGL canvas → toDataURL() → PNG data URL
Performance cost: buffer preservation increases memory usage

steps.title: Stability Analysis: CFL Condition
steps.context: Introduces stability analysis for explicit time integration schemes through the Courant-Friedrichs-Lewy condition.
key_equations: CFL condition: Δt ≤ h²/(4D) for 2D diffusion
Stability constraint: ensures information propagation doesn't exceed grid resolution
Critical timestep: Δt_max = 0.25 × h² / D_max where D_max = max(Du, Dv)

viz.type: Interactive D3 stability diagram
viz.stack: D3.js stability regions, parameter sliders, blowup animation
viz.shows: CFL stability boundary and consequences of violation

test.assertion: CFL stability analysis with interactive parameter control

steps.title: Numerical Instability Demo: dt Too Large
steps.context: Provides hands-on demonstration of CFL condition violation through deliberate timestep increase beyond stability limit.
key_equations: Violation condition: dt > h²/(4D) → exponential growth
Instability growth: |error| ∝ exp(λt) where λ > 0 for unstable modes
Blowup detection: max(|u|, |v|) > threshold or isNaN(u)

viz.type: GPU simulation with deliberate instability
viz.stack: WebGL simulation, timestep control, instability animation, automatic reset
viz.shows: Pattern explosion due to CFL violation with dramatic visual feedback

test.assertion: Controlled instability demonstration with automatic recovery

steps.title: RK4 Integration: Four-Stage Derivation
steps.context: Introduces fourth-order Runge-Kutta integration theory with detailed mathematical derivation and implementation strategy.
key_equations: k₁ = dt × f(u_n, v_n) — slope at beginning
k₂ = dt × f(u_n + k₁/2, v_n + k₁/2) — slope at midpoint using k₁
k₃ = dt × f(u_n + k₂/2, v_n + k₂/2) — slope at midpoint using k₂
k₄ = dt × f(u_n + k₃, v_n + k₃) — slope at end using k₃
u_{n+1} = u_n + (k₁ + 2k₂ + 2k₃ + k₄)/6 — weighted combination

viz.type: Interactive D3 mathematical derivation
viz.stack: D3.js stage visualization, slope diagrams, error analysis charts
viz.shows: Step-by-step RK4 derivation with geometric interpretation

test.assertion: Interactive RK4 derivation with stage-by-stage visualization

steps.title: RK4 on the GPU: Four Ping-Pong Passes
steps.context: Demonstrates GPU implementation of RK4 integration requiring multiple framebuffers and ping-pong rendering passes.
key_equations: Pass 1: k₁ = computeDerivatives(u_n, v_n)
Pass 2: k₂ = computeDerivatives(u_n + 0.5×k₁, v_n + 0.5×k₁)
Pass 3: k₃ = computeDerivatives(u_n + 0.5×k₂, v_n + 0.5×k₂)
Pass 4: k₄ = computeDerivatives(u_n + k₃, v_n + k₃)
Final: u_{n+1} = u_n + (k₁ + 2k₂ + 2k₃ + k₄)/6

viz.type: GPU simulation with RK4 integration
viz.stack: WebGL RK4 shader, 6-FBO architecture, performance monitoring
viz.shows: High-precision pattern evolution with RK4 accuracy

test.assertion: GPU RK4 simulation with 4-pass rendering pipeline

steps.title: Euler vs RK4: Accuracy Comparison
steps.context: Provides side-by-side comparison of Euler and RK4 integration methods with identical initial conditions and parameter sets.
key_equations: Euler error: O(dt) — linear accumulation
RK4 error: O(dt⁴) — quartic scaling advantage
Equivalent accuracy: dt_RK4 ≈ 4 × dt_Euler
Performance ratio: RK4_cost / Euler_cost ≈ 6× memory, 4× compute

viz.type: Dual GPU simulation with method comparison
viz.stack: WebGL split-screen, synchronized parameter control, error tracking
viz.shows: Side-by-side Euler vs RK4 with identical conditions revealing accuracy differences

test.assertion: Split-screen Euler vs RK4 comparison with synchronized parameters

steps.title: Adaptive Time Stepping
steps.context: Introduces adaptive timestep algorithms using step-doubling and Richardson extrapolation for automatic error control.
key_equations: Step doubling: compute u(t+2h) using single step vs two h-steps
Richardson error estimate: |error| ≈ |u_2h - u_h|/15 for RK4
Adaptive control: dt_new = dt_old × (tolerance/error)^(1/5)
Safety factor: 0.8 ≤ scaling ≤ 2.0 to prevent thrashing

viz.type: GPU simulation with adaptive timestep control
viz.stack: WebGL simulation, dual-step error estimation, timestep history display
viz.shows: Automatic timestep adaptation responding to pattern complexity

test.assertion: Adaptive timestep simulation with automatic error control

steps.title: Spatial Resolution: 128 vs 512 vs 1024
steps.context: Compares pattern formation at different grid resolutions to demonstrate spatial resolution effects on pattern wavelength and detail.
key_equations: Nyquist limit: λ_min ≈ 4h where h is grid spacing
Resolution scaling: 1024² requires 64× memory vs 128²
Pattern wavelength: λ ∝ √(Du/k) — intrinsic length scale
Grid requirement: N > 4λ/L for proper resolution

viz.type: Multi-resolution GPU simulation comparison
viz.stack: WebGL multi-viewport, resolution controls, memory monitoring
viz.shows: Pattern detail vs grid resolution with performance metrics

steps.title: Anti-Aliasing: Bilinear vs Nearest Sampling
steps.context: Explores WebGL texture filtering modes and their impact on both visualization quality and numerical simulation accuracy.
key_equations: Bilinear interpolation: value = (1-fx)(1-fy)×c00 + fx(1-fy)×c10 + ...
Nearest sampling: value = texture[floor(u×width), floor(v×height)]
Numerical dissipation: bilinear filtering acts like artificial viscosity
Aliasing frequency: sampling < 2×pattern frequency → artifacts

viz.type: GPU simulation with texture filtering comparison
viz.stack: WebGL simulation, dual filtering modes, zoom controls, aliasing demonstration
viz.shows: Visual and numerical differences between texture filtering modes

test.assertion: Texture filtering comparison showing visual and numerical differences

steps.title: Mass Conservation Check
steps.context: Implements mass conservation monitoring as a diagnostic tool for detecting numerical errors and implementation problems.
key_equations: Total mass: M(t) = ∬(U + V)dA over entire domain
Conservation property: dM/dt = f×∬(1-U)dA - (f+k)×∬V dA < 0
Numerical check: M(t+dt) ≤ M(t) must hold for all timesteps
Error detection: |dM/dt - expected| > tolerance indicates problems

code_functions.name: mass integration
code_functions.purpose: GPU reduction for total mass calculation

steps.title: Convergence Study
steps.context: Implements Richardson extrapolation-based convergence analysis to verify spatial discretization accuracy and implementation correctness.
key_equations: Richardson extrapolation: u_exact ≈ u_h + (u_h - u_2h)/(2^p - 1)
Convergence order: p = log₂[(u_4h - u_2h)/(u_2h - u_h)]
Expected order: p ≈ 2 for centered finite differences
Error scaling: |error| ∝ h^p where p is convergence order

viz.type: D3 convergence analysis with error scaling plots
viz.stack: D3.js error plots, multi-resolution data, Richardson analysis
viz.shows: Error vs grid spacing confirming O(h²) convergence

test.assertion: Convergence study showing O(h²) spatial accuracy

steps.title: Anisotropic Diffusion Tensor
steps.context: Extends isotropic diffusion to full tensor form enabling directional diffusion effects and anisotropic pattern formation.
key_equations: Tensor diffusion: ∂u/∂t = ∇·(D∇u) where D is 2×2 tensor
Matrix form: D = [Dxx, Dxy; Dyx, Dyy] with Dxy = Dyx
Principal directions: eigenvectors of D determine preferred axes
Anisotropy ratio: λ_max/λ_min controls directional strength

viz.stack: WebGL tensor diffusion shader, tensor visualization overlay, parameter controls
viz.shows: Directional pattern growth with tensor field visualization

steps.title: Noise Perturbation: Gaussian Noise on U
steps.context: Introduces stochastic perturbations to break symmetry and accelerate Turing pattern formation through noise-driven nucleation.
key_equations: Stochastic term: dU/dt = ... + σ×ξ(x,t) where ξ is white noise
Noise amplitude: σ controls perturbation strength
Correlation: ⟨ξ(x,t)ξ(x',t')⟩ = δ(x-x')δ(t-t')
Symmetry breaking: uniform states become unstable to noise

viz.stack: WebGL simulation, GPU PRNG, noise visualization, nucleation timing

key_equations: Parameter fields: f = f(x,y) and k = k(x,y) as spatial functions
Gradient effects: ∇f and ∇k create pattern drift and boundary conditions
Pattern pinning: sharp parameter changes can trap or reflect patterns
Texture mapping: parameters sampled from GPU textures for efficiency

steps.title: Multi-Scale Simulation
steps.context: Implements coupled coarse-fine grid simulation to demonstrate scale separation and hierarchical pattern formation.
key_equations: Coarse grid: U_coarse, V_coarse at resolution N×N
Fine grid: U_fine, V_fine at resolution 4N×4N
Upscaling: U_coarse = ∫U_fine dA over coarse cells
Downscaling: U_fine interpolated from U_coarse at boundaries

steps.title: 3D Extension Concept: Volumetric RD
steps.context: Explores conceptual extension to 3D volumetric reaction-diffusion with discussion of computational challenges and pattern types.
key_equations: 3D Laplacian: ∇²u = ∂²u/∂x² + ∂²u/∂y² + ∂²u/∂z²
6-neighbor stencil: u_{i,j,k±1} contributions in all three directions
Memory scaling: N³ grows rapidly vs N² for 2D
Pattern types: tubes, sheets, gyroid minimal surfaces

steps.title: 3D Surface: Vertex Displacement by U
steps.context: Transforms 2D Gray-Scott concentration field into 3D heightfield surface for enhanced pattern topology visualization.
key_equations: Height mapping: z = A × U(x,y) where A is amplitude scaling
Gradient magnitude: |∇U| = √((∂U/∂x)² + (∂U/∂y)²)
Curvature: κ = ∇²U at each point

steps.title: Turing Instability Analysis: Linearization
steps.context: Introduces linear stability analysis to predict Turing pattern onset and characteristic wavelengths from system parameters.
key_equations: Linearization: u = u₀ + εe^(σt+ikx), v = v₀ + εe^(σt+ikx)
Dispersion relation: σ(k²) determines growth rates vs wavenumber
Instability condition: σ > 0 for unstable modes
Critical wavenumber: k_c where σ(k_c²) = 0, maximum growth

viz.type: D3 dispersion relation analysis with parameter exploration
viz.stack: D3.js mathematical plotting, parameter sliders, stability analysis
viz.shows: Growth rate vs wavenumber revealing unstable modes and predicted wavelengths

test.assertion: Linear stability analysis with dispersion relation visualization

steps.title: Pattern Wavelength: Dispersion Relation
steps.context: Connects theoretical dispersion relations to observed pattern wavelengths through direct measurement and comparison.
key_equations: Wavelength prediction: λ* = 2π/k* where σ'(k*²) = 0
Fourier analysis: k* = dominant wavenumber in |FFT(u)|²
Parameter dependence: λ* ∝ √(Du/f) approximate scaling
Measurement accuracy: FFT resolution limits k-space sampling

viz.type: D3 spectral analysis with theoretical comparison
viz.stack: D3.js FFT visualization, spectral analysis, wavelength measurement
viz.shows: Measured vs predicted pattern wavelengths confirming dispersion theory

test.assertion: Pattern wavelength analysis with theoretical validation

steps.title: Symmetry Breaking: Sensitivity to ICs
steps.context: Explores how different initial conditions lead to the same attractor but with different spatial organization and symmetry properties.
key_equations: Attractor convergence: all ICs → same pattern type (spots, stripes, etc.)
Spatial organization: sensitive to IC symmetries and perturbations
Symmetry inheritance: patterns often reflect initial condition symmetries
Basin boundaries: IC space divided by final pattern organization

code_functions.name: multi-IC simulation
code_functions.purpose: Parallel simulations with different initial conditions

viz.type: Multi-panel GPU simulation with IC comparison
viz.stack: WebGL multi-viewport simulation, IC generation tools, pattern comparison
viz.shows: Different initial conditions converging to similar patterns with different organization

test.assertion: Multi-IC simulation showing attractor convergence and symmetry effects

steps.title: Coupling Two Gray-Scott Layers
steps.context: Introduces coupled Gray-Scott layers where each layer influences the other through cross-coupling terms.
key_equations: Layer 1: ∂u₁/∂t = Du₁∇²u₁ - u₁v₁² + f₁(1-u₁) + C₁₂(u₂-u₁)
Layer 2: ∂u₂/∂t = Du₂∇²u₂ - u₂v₂² + f₂(1-u₂) + C₂₁(u₁-u₂)
Cross-coupling: C₁₂, C₂₁ control inter-layer influence strength
Emergent dynamics: synchronization, competition, wave propagation

viz.type: Dual-layer GPU simulation with coupling visualization
viz.stack: WebGL dual-buffer simulation, coupling visualization, synchronization analysis
viz.shows: Coupled pattern dynamics with emergent spatio-temporal behaviors

test.assertion: Coupled dual-layer simulation with emergent pattern interactions

steps.title: WebGL Performance Best Practices
steps.context: Implements WebGL optimization strategies for maximum rendering performance in scientific computing applications.
key_equations: Frame budget: 16.67ms per frame for 60fps target
State change cost: context switches dominate GPU performance
Buffer reuse: minimize GL buffer allocation/deallocation
Draw call batching: fewer calls with more primitives preferred

viz.type: Optimized GPU simulation with performance monitoring
viz.stack: WebGL optimized pipeline, performance profiling, frame timing analysis
viz.shows: High-performance simulation maintaining 60fps with complex patterns

test.assertion: Optimized WebGL pipeline maintaining target frame rate

steps.title: Sub-Stepping for Speed
steps.context: Implements variable sub-stepping to maximize GPU computational throughput while maintaining smooth visualization frame rates.
key_equations: Sub-step ratio: N = floor(16.67ms / simulation_time_per_step)
Effective speedup: N× faster evolution for same visual frame rate
GPU utilization: maximize texture bandwidth and shader throughput
Display decimation: show every Nth simulation frame

viz.type: Sub-stepped GPU simulation with speed monitoring
viz.stack: WebGL multi-step pipeline, performance analysis, speedup visualization
viz.shows: Accelerated pattern evolution through sub-stepping optimization

test.assertion: Sub-stepped simulation showing significant speed improvement

steps.title: Web Worker CPU Fallback
steps.context: Implements Web Worker-based CPU simulation as fallback for WebGL-incompatible devices and browsers.
key_equations: CPU Laplacian: same finite difference stencil as GPU version
Memory transfer: SharedArrayBuffer avoids data copying overhead
Thread communication: postMessage for control, SharedArrayBuffer for data
Performance scaling: CPU ~100× slower than GPU for same resolution

viz.type: Text-based fallback status and performance comparison
viz.stack: Web Worker simulation, SharedArrayBuffer, fallback detection
viz.shows: CPU simulation performance comparison and compatibility reporting

test.assertion: Web Worker CPU simulation with SharedArrayBuffer communication

steps.title: Responsive Layout: Resize and DPR
steps.context: Implements responsive WebGL canvas sizing with proper handling of device pixel ratio for high-quality rendering across devices.
key_equations: Canvas sizing: canvas.width = displayWidth × devicePixelRatio
Aspect ratio: maintain simulation aspect ratio during resize
DPR scaling: CSS pixels vs device pixels for high-DPI displays
Performance impact: resolution scales quadratically with DPR

viz.type: Responsive GPU simulation with DPR handling
viz.stack: WebGL responsive canvas, ResizeObserver, DPR detection, aspect ratio preservation
viz.shows: Seamless resize behavior maintaining simulation quality across device types

test.assertion: Responsive layout with proper resize and DPR handling

steps.title: URL Hash Navigation: Deep Linking
steps.context: Implements URL-based state management for bookmarkable and shareable simulation configurations.
key_equations: State encoding: parameters → base64 → URL hash fragment
Deep linking: URL → parameters → simulation state restoration
History management: pushState for back/forward browser navigation
State compression: minimize URL length while preserving precision

viz.type: Text-based URL state management demonstration
viz.stack: URL hash parsing, history API, state serialization
viz.shows: URL-based parameter sharing and bookmark functionality

test.assertion: URL hash navigation with state persistence and sharing

steps.title: Progress Persistence: localStorage
steps.context: Implements client-side progress tracking and persistence using localStorage for seamless cross-session user experience.
key_equations: Storage capacity: ~5MB localStorage limit per origin
Data serialization: JSON.stringify/parse for structured data
Persistence lifetime: survives browser restart, lost on cache clear
Compression strategy: delta encoding for large state objects

viz.type: Text-based progress tracking and localStorage management
viz.stack: localStorage API, JSON serialization, progress indicators
viz.shows: Persistent progress tracking across browser sessions

test.assertion: Progress persistence with localStorage across browser sessions

steps.title: Vite Production Build: Asset Hashing
steps.context: Implements production build configuration with asset hashing and optimization for deployment to CDN-enabled hosting platforms.
key_equations: Cache invalidation: filename hash changes → browser cache miss
Compression ratio: gzip typically 70-80% size reduction
Bundle splitting: separate vendor chunks for better caching
Hash stability: content-based hashing for maximum cache efficiency

viz.type: Text-based build analysis and deployment configuration
viz.stack: Vite build system, asset hashing, bundle analysis
viz.shows: Production build optimization and CDN preparation

test.assertion: Production build with proper asset hashing and optimization

steps.title: Cloudflare Pages Deployment
steps.context: Demonstrates deployment to Cloudflare Pages using wrangler CLI with global CDN distribution and edge optimization.
key_equations: Global latency: edge servers reduce RTT to <50ms worldwide
Cache hit ratio: static assets cached at edge with 99.9% hit rate
Bandwidth scaling: CDN handles traffic spikes without origin load
Cold start elimination: static hosting = zero server initialization delay

viz.type: Text-based deployment guide and configuration management
viz.stack: Cloudflare Pages, wrangler CLI, global CDN deployment
viz.shows: Complete deployment process from build to global availability

test.assertion: Successful deployment to Cloudflare Pages with global CDN

steps.title: Performance Audit: Lighthouse + WebGL
steps.context: Implements comprehensive performance auditing combining web performance metrics with WebGL-specific profiling and optimization.
key_equations: Performance budget: FCP<1s, TTI<2s, FPS≥60, frame time≤16.67ms
GPU profiling: gl.getError(), performance.now() for timing
Memory monitoring: WebGLRenderingContext memory usage tracking
Optimization targets: Lighthouse score >90, WebGL frame drops <1%

viz.type: Performance monitoring dashboard with WebGL metrics
viz.stack: Lighthouse API, Stats.js profiling, WebGL performance counters
viz.shows: Comprehensive performance metrics and optimization recommendations

test.assertion: Performance audit achieving target metrics for web and WebGL

steps.title: Final Synthesis: Full Gray-Scott System
steps.context: Integrates all course elements into a comprehensive Gray-Scott simulation platform with advanced features and deployment-ready architecture.
key_equations: Complete system: all Gray-Scott PDEs with extensions
All visualizations: 2D/3D, analysis tools, export capabilities
All optimizations: GPU, CPU fallback, performance monitoring
All deployment: CDN, responsive design, persistence

viz.type: Complete Gray-Scott platform with all visualization modes
viz.stack: Full technology integration, all features from steps 1-99
viz.shows: Comprehensive reaction-diffusion simulation platform suitable for research and education

test.assertion: Complete Gray-Scott system with all features functional
